_comment: Permission rules the plugin REQUIRES for the §3.7 single-permission contract and the §3.3 deploy-safety gates to function. Plugin-level settings.json's `permissions` block is silently ignored by Claude Code (verified iter-12 audit, 2026-05-02), so these rules can't ship as plugin defaults. The /lua-doctor offers to merge this into the user's `.claude/settings.json` (project-level) on first run; the user accepts once and the rules persist for the project. If the rules are absent at runtime, the plugin still works but: every safe Bash invocation triggers a permission prompt (degrades §3.7 contract), and the deploy-deny rule doesn't fire (degrades §3.3 defence-in-depth — the hook still gates, but as the only line of defence rather than the second).
